content:
Reality Verges are the little globes you get every day for completing daily quests, they’re used primairly in unlocking side stories, reproduction frenzies and unlocking miscellanous things in the Archive.

Now with the implementation of the handbook, players are suggested a set order in which to read the game’s story… howhever due to the way verges are structured unless you’re logging in daily and decide to do only one chapter a month there will come a point in which you simply can’t read all the stories in any given chapter section, which while a lot of these aren’t needed to understand the plot of the main story; new players likely won’t know this and those who do will just feel bad about having to skip stories especially if they were particularly invested in the story

As for solutions,  probably the most obvious one would be to outright remove the verge requirement for these stories howhever that comes with the second edge of giving the player way too many posses that they should have that early in the game. Maybe the *stories* unlock automatically as you pass by them in the handbook but you still need to pay Verges to unlock the *posses*

I agree, also right now the only way to speed up story unlocks is the premium version of the curriculum pass, but time gating the story and charging money to unlock it faster is a really bad look from the perspective of new players. There are other material rewards from the pass, so it doesn't really feel like the current system is holding the story hostage or anything, but it still kind of has that appearance at first glance
just to add to this: if you only decide to unlock the stories recommended by Niko's guides, that's 60+ reality verges you need to unlock all the needed story so at least 2 months of daily logins
give 5 reality verges per day , do it !!
I honestly agree with making the stories free from Verges but keeping the posses locked behind them, if that is the intention of story locked behind verges
especially for new players it's really not a good look and considering the devs want to appeal to more people, this definitely doesn't help their cause
Oh that is fascinating
Agreed with this!
i hope the devs can see and address this soon
although i reckon they already have a lot on their plate
No speedrun, bad Angbak bad
aurita text still not updated, also when aurita +12 buff ?

After you reset the event shop often enough that the limited items are gone, this warning serves no purpose anymore. If people didn't read it the first 5 times it was displayed, they won't read it afterwards either and still wonder why their limited items won't refresh.
So after all shop refreshes are used, that message should not display. I would even suggest to just auto-refresh when all items are gone or let me turn in my whole currency stack in one go.

It's a slight inconvenience, but it's probably easy to fix and it will feel just right to have fewer steps to turn in stuff after you pressed the single turn in button often enough.

This is kinda funny because `1%` is technically, `0.01`, so `0.25%` is in fact, `0.0025%`

But it is possible the equation is wrong, yes.

i think the main point fjant is trying to get at is the 25% bonus not being added directly to the initial 75% boost, but rather the rm boost only affecting the 25% bonus, rather than being its own boost

the first equation is ``24 * 0.25%`` so it's not a difference between the % value calcs
(Yeah, after reading the math they were showing some more, I was like "huh")
yeah the equation is really interesting
since wording implies that the 25% is added onto the initial
then there's a different bonus added by realm mastery
but the calc seems to be saying that the second rm bonus is added on to the 25%, which is multiplicative with the initial 75% amp bonus
wait i gotta do graphs on this
i don't think i got anything wrong, since it's calcing 0.25% **of** the rm as the added bonus to the multiplicative 25%, while the first one is just raw percentages in an additive format
but that makes it really really weird after a certain point yeah
like at 50 rm it becomes 112.5 amp vs 103.125 amp
and the gap only grows

+0.5 from Covenant Piece, +12 from half-Covenant, +36, from Wheel 1, +18 from Wheel 2, effect is +30%, and +15% from Pursuit

12+36+18, -> 66.5RM

Exalting gives +25%, the bonus should be +66*.025, = 16.5%

Total bonus is then 41.5%, on top of the base.

But the thing that I'm not understanding, is my Exalt skill is 98, but this isn't in game, it's on the Awakener page, so it shouldn't be calculated as such.

So it may be that the tooltip itself is wrong.
because the second equation will always **always** be calcing from 125% **of** the initial exalt bonus vs the flat 25% increase from the first equation (which equals 133% of initial exalt)
the thing is, if i increase the exalt level/exalt base, the gap decreases, because the second equation has a larger scaling platform to go from
(i plugged in your 98% exalt amp)
yeah I'm not sure... what the heck is happening here
It feels like the math is incorrect, like from the base
if i make the base exalt amp 100%, then the lines are perfectly even
because then the game calcs it the same
but if the exalt amp is < 100, the current equation gives a lower yield than the expected (1st equation), and vice versa for if the base exalt amp is > 100
just because of how the equation work
smh guess we gotta demand free rolls for bad math
(kidding)
hope it gets fixed tho
yeah the e1 wording implies it adds flat 25% dmg amp directly onto the pre-existing amp, but the math is saying it adds 25% **of** the pre-existing amp onto itself

Expected from wording:
``75% + 25% + 24 * 0.25% = 106%``

Observed/math result (rounding not included):
``75% + (75(25% + 24 * 0.25%)) = 98.25%``

translated the second equation into percentage equivalent to look at more closely
Yea sorry I was getting confused explaining it myself so it was a chatgpt explain why the math is weird angle xd
Hmm i got the feedback from the TH community but i think they might be wrong about clem, i think not seeing thd number go up for the silver key might be misleading
Lemme check foutte real quick
<@1470253170209853632> this is for fouette, confirmed not working
The wod is at e12
No wait lemme recheck again
Yep foutte doesnt activate at the end of the ultra round

for this in particular, during the ultra turn you remove the stacks, but what you get depedns on the tipe of them, the damage ones give you 50 keyflare each, as you can see you got 750 keyflare when you entered ultra turn. 
And you also get the correct aliemus in the second video, so it seems to work correctly
yeah, that wod is not working

Waiting for 2.5.1 buff. Day 6

Hi, with another suggestion and this is for the events


I'm not sure if it has been discussed, but events and the design of the events levels should be designed more clear or more separated and easy to understand for new players

As a new player, i went into the rewards and saw the events, thinking it was some progression bar and was looking everywhere else for the events (but tbh it looks like a me problem)

the current abstract idea I have, is that events should be on another screen tab on the home menu 


I just wanted to share my thoughts, if you guys are open to UX design ideas I'm down to share some

some issues for an example, the top "left" says rewards tab when some of these are events

and in the level design, it looks like some progression bar for each tier reward in my perspective

Hiya, I just wanted to reach out and suggest that the Mythag shop music, should be the default music for the home-screen

as of current it is not possible to set it in the records

The main point i am trying to make, is that current home screen is too eerie and not relaxing, or well too hard on the ears when someone is trying to read all the content of the game

i mean, if one care about the story, especially the early chapter, it will probably play it in not too much time, so the option to oped just the story part of the side stories is actually a pretty nice qol that is just a positive

it takes TWO months just to unlock the recommended side stories in Nikos guide. this isn't a matter of "don't rush yourself" because even a slow reader would be done with the current story before he gets 60 reality verges
it's a qol that strictly benefits the players, doesn't lose the devs any money and let's you enjoy the story and characters, i don't get why being hostile towards it?
i tried recommending the game to a few of my friends and this feature turned them off even when they had already showed interest in the series
Quite fast turnoff ngl
sure, if you only play the game for the gameplay but the story is what interested them
Yea it’s calculating an added 25% **of** the initial bonus, with the “25%” being the only thing affected by rm

So does the AA do anything or is just a nothing burger?

-# I don't even have her

I'm just interested

the game makes it very easy to progress through the early story chapters quickly, even without rushing, but they just added the handbook which organizes the side events by main story chapter, and it would take 59 days to unlock the stories they list for the first 2 main story chapters. Even if most of those aren't important to the main story, new players *don't know that* and they just see a bunch of story stuff recommended for the chapter they just finished that it would take them months to unlock. And even the side stories that *are* important to the main story requiring delaying the main story by a few weeks to fully unlock.

I don't understand why you'd be opposed to removing barriers to entry to the game, new players running into blocks when trying to read the main story at their own pace is a bad thing. The game *needs* new players, there's no reason not to make the story more accessible
I just don't see an issue if they do them out of order.

Cause then you can have that

"Oh wait this is before X"

point is, from the perspective of new players it *is* an issue, people don't want to play the story out of order if they don't have to. Just because it's fine to you as a long time player, doesn't mean it's not a point of friction for people just starting the game, and it hurts no one to adjust it. So, coming in to say 'well it's not a problem to me' when a lot of people have expressed why it's a problem to them just seems unnecessarily dismissive

It's fine 
24 has already 3 realms where she is great 
4 would be pushing it 
It's okay if she is not great in Blue
Is not a major problem, but is a thing that people that like story and lore would care about, and the change seems reasonable and feasible, so why not consider them?

I'm kinda having a tough time following what are you trying to convey, because you tagged this thread as bug report and games suggestions?

You need to make a statement what are you trying to say and what is your proposed solution, all these facts you wrote down are not facts and are opinions, you need to separate them to be more convincing and make your message clear

the reason why i say this, is because a quick counter argument to your inquiry dismiss what you are trying to convey and that is:

rewards are for your efforts, not because you created an account, you don't fret over rewards that are out of your reach or events and opportunities you missed, so why should it apply for the bonuses on PVP?

People are not forced to play PvP and PVP is a dying game-mode with no players, even eng community guides for it stopped being updated

Also meaning there should be more rewards for people who make an effort to go out of their way to play and try that game mode despite not being mandatory  and for fun, it gives incentive not pressure because you also get ranked accordingly

While their arguments aren't the best organized it is pretty clear they feel the devs are putting PVE rewards into PVP with the express purpose of trying to force players to engage with PVP.

As for a solution they imply the way it worked before was fine with PVP being entirely seperate mode with limited PVE rewards and the main rewards being the emotes.

"force players to engage with PvP"

 is misleading and disingenuous 

there is incentive but there is no force making them engage with PVP

To add to this, for all-pick, you would also need to unlock the characters to be used in that queue, 

while draft mode allowes people to get a taste of a character design and playstyle to an extend, explore something totally different 

The rewards are better and make sense in a way to add an incentive that benefits both PVP and PVE, but i would argue to increase the incentive because the game mode is really a low amount of player pool for such a new take on the game, like it is a different game itself

You are the one being disingenuous. Putting rewards that only effect PVE and not PVP behind a PVP mode that is entirely different from the PVE game is clearly an attempt to use FOMO to try and boost the PVP playerbase. Why would PVP rewards not be related to PVP? Obviously, a signifianct amount of the playerbase has no interest in PVP as there have been many post sabout disapointment in the 2.5 changes.
can you clarify what is the rewards that only effect PVE?

Soulforce mats from ranking are PVE only. Sure, you need to technically pull to unlock all charcters in PVP but you only need 1 copy so the amount of pulls needed is far less that what is needed for PVE account progression.

and also, im not here to name call and berate each other, we are here to solve and give constructive feedback to their team together as fellow players, 

Act professional, i have never worded attacks against your character which is why i quoted your words and not you

I'm sorry but that is not name calling and is no different then you calling my argment misleadeing and disingenuous. Your intial comment came across as dismissive of the various FOMO tactics that are too often used in gacha games and saying players aren't "forced" to do something is a common way to dismiss these tactics while ignoring the inherent purpose of them.

For new players, like myself, the rewards they give are beneficial because they are sided in helping with, what we both stated, unlocking characters with the Silver rewards

from what i can see to support your argument, the rank placements dream shard and dream spark are PVE focused, but they give very little compared to what the Limited Events on the basis of "effort and value" it is terrible and not worth the grind in comparison 

otherwise i would have to respectfully disagree and say there needs to be more incentive for people who go out of their way for this dying game-mode and i would say replace the PVE focused rewards with silver

it is the fact of the matter trying to obtain the rewards is optional and you dont need them to enjoy the game; it is not dismissing the issue and you are playing with yourself and contradict your statements and even backtrack to what you said a response later, im not going to bother to respond to you no more, you can't even bother to think before you type, you can doom post and fear monger more it won't help your lack of persuasion and genuineness especially to the people and the devs who take their time of day to read your statements coming off as a weak-compliant instead of something to work off
Yes, I don't think we are going to see eye to eye on this so best to end it here.
